Key Takeaways:
o The Pre-Prelaunch Quickstart Guide is a quick overview of the entire Pre-
Prelaunch process.
o The Pre-Prelaunch comes before your 3 PLCs (which don’t have to be videos)
o The Shot Across the Bow is a naval term for a warning shot and in your Pre-
Prelaunch – it’s a way to let your list know something is coming.
o You can use the Pre-Prelaunch to:
o Warm your list
o Help you figure out primary objections
o Craft and refine your product and offer (most applicable if you have an
information based product)
o Gather testimonials
o Creates anticipation and buzz
o Attract JV Partners
o There’s a checklist in the Quickstart Guide but you don’t need to do everything on it
– it’s just a list of all the things you might possibly do.
o The Pre-Pre doesn’t have to be and shouldn’t be complicated – it’s super effective
and really easy to do.
o Download the guide and leave a comment on what you do with it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The Pre-Prelaunch, this is your Quick Start Guide, this is just a quick guide. What
you want to think about with your Pre-Pre? The Pre-Prelaunch as you see right here in this
diagram is what comes before your Prelaunch, before your Prelaunch Content, before your
three videos. You don't have to do videos, I have stressed that over and over. It comes before
PLC1, PLC2, PLC3, your Pre-Prelaunch.
This is what I call the Shot Across the Bow which is this old naval term for a warning
shot. If you want to let another ship know that you mean business, you fire a shot but you
shoot it in front of them. That's what the Pre-Prelaunch is - it's just sort of a way of letting
your prospects, your market know that something is coming. You don't have to do the Pre-
Pre, but I really like to do it because it's usually not involved, not complicated, it's often a
single email. Maybe it's a single email and a survey, but it's just simple, it's easy, but it has
some very effective things that it can do for you, and here's a list.
It can warm your list - it almost always will serve that purpose.
It helps you figure out some of the primary objections in your market.
It helps you craft and refine your product and offer. This is most applicable if you
have an information-based product and you can easily tweak that even as you go into your
launch. Even if you have a physical widget, you're doing e-Commerce or something like that,
or you have an offer that's really locked in, you can often add things, tweak things, add
information-based components to it. Even in your Internal Launch, the Pre-Pre can help you
refine what you want to offer.
My famous testimonial cruncher is part of this.
This isn't something you have to do but it's been very, very, very effective. It's just
helps you judge the market, judge how interested they are.
This is a big one - it creates anticipation and buzz before you're even selling anything,
before you're even in Prelaunch.
It might even help you attract JV partners. This isn’t a principal reason to do a Pre-
Pre, but just sort of that Shot Across the Bow can tend to attract people.
In this Quick Start Guide, we walk through some of the basics for you. You definitely
should download this and check it out. It's just a great intro to it.
Also we gave you this big checklist. This is really, really, really important. You do
NOT need to do all these things. In fact, you don't even have to do a Pre-Pre, but you do not
have to do all these things every time. I just wanted to give you a big list of things you could
possibly do… so that's the checklist.
This is the Pre-Pre, again, it doesn't have to be complicated, it shouldn't be
complicated. It's something I really like to do, you don't have to do but it can really ... It gives
you a lot of warm, fuzzy feelings going into your Prelaunch, it can be super effective and it's
really easy to do. This is your Quick Start Guide, definitely check it out, and I look forward to
hearing what you do with it.
